words.def: good things that happen to you by chance, not because of your own efforts or abilities
words.example: with (any) luck, With any luck, we'll be home before dark.

words.def: having good luck
words.example: lucky to do something, His friend was killed and he knows he is lucky to be alive.

words.def: the official system used for sending and delivering letters, packages, etc.
very large or important
to control or be in charge of a business, a team, an organization, land, etc.
a person who is in charge of running a business, a shop or a similar organization or part of one
behaviour that is considered to be polite in a particular society or culture
a written or printed symbol that is used as a sign of something, for example the quality of something or who made or owns it
having a husband or wife
to become the husband or wife of somebody; to get married to somebody
mathematics, especially as a subject in school
words.example: The core subjects are English, maths and science.

words.def: used (to ask) if somebody is upset, unhappy, etc. or if there is a problem
words.example: What's the matter? Is there something wrong?

words.def: the fifth month of the year, between April and June
to have something as a meaning in the same or another language
words.example: mean something, What does this sentence mean?

words.def: the thing or idea that a sound, word, sign, etc. represents
words.example: What's the meaning of this word?

words.def: connected with illness and injury and their treatment
the study and treatment of diseases and injuries
a person, an animal or a plant that belongs to a particular group
words.example: member of something, a member of the family/community

words.def: your ability to remember things
words.example: memory for something, I have a bad memory for names.

words.def: a list of the food that is available at a restaurant or to be served at a meal
a type of solid mineral substance that is usually hard and shiny and that heat and electricity can travel through, for example tin, iron and gold
a particular way of doing something
words.example: Which method is the most effective?

words.def: a unit for measuring length; a hundred centimetres
in a position in the middle of an object, group of objects, people, etc.; between the beginning and the end of something
words.example: Pens are kept in the middle drawer.

words.def: 12 o’clock at night
words.example: She heard the clock strike midnight.

words.def: used when showing that something is or was possible
words.example: He might get there in time, but I can't be sure.
a population of half a million

words.def: the part of a person that makes them able to be aware of things, to think and to feel
a piece of special flat glass that reflects images, so that you can see yourself when you look in it
words.example: in the mirror, He looked at himself in the mirror.

words.def: that cannot be found or that is not in its usual place; that has been removed, lost or destroyed
words.example: I never found the missing piece.

words.def: connected with mobile phones, tablets, etc.
of the present time or recent times
an animal with a long tail, that climbs trees and lives in hot countries. There are several types of monkey and they are related to apes and humans.
words.example: Like humans, apes and monkeys live in complex social groupings.

words.def: the round object that moves around the earth once every 27½ days and shines at night by light reflected from the sun
used to form the superlative of most adjectives and adverbs of two or more syllables
mainly; generally
a building in which objects of artistic, cultural, historical or scientific interest are kept and shown to the public
a person who plays a musical instrument or writes music, especially as a job
used when the speaker or writer is also the person affected by an action
words.example: I cut myself on a knife.

words.def: existing in nature; not made or caused by humans
words.example: the natural world (= of trees, rivers, animals and birds)

words.def: all the plants, animals and things that exist in the universe that are not made by people
words.example: Take time to appreciate the beauties of nature.

words.def: almost; not quite; not completely
that is needed for a purpose or a reason
words.example: necessary to do something, It may be necessary to buy a new one.

words.def: the part of the body between the head and the shoulders
words.example: He tied a scarf around his neck.

words.def: anxious about something or afraid of something
words.example: I felt really nervous before the interview.

words.def: not anyone; no person
a sound, especially when it is loud, unpleasant or frightening
words.example: There was a rattling noise coming from the back of the car.

words.def: making a lot of noise
in or towards the north
a sheet of paper giving written or printed information, usually put in a public place
words.example: There was a notice on the board saying the class had been cancelled.

words.def: a word or symbol that represents an amount or a quantity
words.example: Think of a number and multiply it by two.

words.def: an act of saying that you are willing to do something for somebody or give something to somebody
a person who is in a position of authority in the armed forces
a thick liquid that is found in rock underground
words.example: Several companies are drilling for oil in the region.

words.def: on somebody’s body; being worn
a round vegetable with many layers inside each other and a brown, red or white skin. Onions have a strong smell and taste.
available on or done using the internet or other computer network
words.example: Online shopping is both cheap and convenient.

words.def: your feelings or thoughts about somebody/something, rather than a fact
words.example: He has very strong political opinions.

words.def: a time when a particular situation makes it possible to do or achieve something
on the other side of a particular area from somebody/something and usually facing them
words.example: Answers are given on the opposite page.

words.def: to make all the arrangements for something to happen or be provided
words.example: He helped to organize various events.

words.def: the reflexive form of we; used when you and another person or other people together cause and are affected by an action
words.example: We shouldn't blame ourselves for what happened.

words.def: the part of a cooker that is like a box with a door on the front, in which food is cooked or heated
words.example: Take the cake out of the oven.

words.def: across a street, an open space, etc.
used to emphasize that something belongs to or is connected with somebody
a person who owns something
a person whose job is painting buildings, walls, etc.
words.example: He works as a painter and decorator.

words.def: the official home of a king, queen, president, etc.
underpants or knickers
some but not all of a thing
words.example: We spent part of the time in the museum.

words.def: a person who is travelling in a car, bus, train, plane or ship and who is not driving it or working on it
the money that somebody gets for doing regular work
words.example: Her job is hard work, but the pay is good.

words.def: a situation or a period of time in which there is no war or violence in a country or an area
a powder made from dried berries (called peppercorns), used to give a hot, spicy taste to food
having everything that is necessary; complete and without faults or weaknesses
to entertain an audience by playing a piece of music, acting in a play, etc.
possibly
words.example: ‘Are you going to come?’ ‘Perhaps. I'll see how I feel.’

words.def: the act of allowing somebody to do something, especially when this is done by somebody in a position of authority
words.example: permission for something, You must ask permission for all major expenditure.

words.def: the various aspects of a person’s character that combine to make them different from other people
words.example: His wife has a strong personality.

words.def: an animal, a bird, etc. that you have at home for pleasure, rather than one that is kept for work or food
a liquid obtained from petroleum, used as fuel in car engines, etc.
words.example: to fill a car up with petrol

words.def: a picture that is made by using a camera that stores images in digital form or that has a film sensitive to light inside it
words.example: aerial photographs of the crash site

words.def: connected with a person’s body rather than their mind
a person who operates the controls of an aircraft, especially as a job
a large round object in space that moves around a star (such as the sun) and receives light from it
words.example: the planets of our solar system
